changes.description: Public profiles — tap any leaderboard card, group run history name or challenge opponent to open their full profile in a modal. Shows stats, Frodo's Journey progress and titles.
Scheduled events now appear in the sidebar widget with the same coming-soon countdown overlay as pending challenges.
Onboarding system — interactive feature tours guide new members through the leaderboard, challenges, events and profile page step by step.
Patch notes — new features trigger an in-app notification so you always know what changed. Full release history on the Features page.
Frodo's Journey expanded with 24 new Tolkien waypoints (Weathertop, Lothlórien, Cirith Ungol and more) and a three-level zoom function to see your local progress in detail.
Journey moved to its own full-width card directly below the profile header for better visibility.

Events system — app-wide challenges that trigger automatically on a schedule or based on real weather. Two types: Participation (earn XP by running within a time window) and Competition (weekly ranking by total km or elevation).

Five participation events: Morning run (06:00–10:00), Evening run (18:00–22:00), 5K Friday, Hangover Run (weekends), and Storm Chaser — only triggered when tomorrow's forecast shows rain, drizzle, thunderstorm or strong gusts.
Two weekly competition events: Weekly km and Weekly höjdmeter — ranking everyone in the group, settled Sunday at 23:55 with XP awarded per place (40/30/20).
Events page — shows active and upcoming events, live competition leaderboard, and full history with per-event results and group completion status.
Event widgets in the sidebar — active participation events show a countdown to close, competition events show your current rank. Upcoming events show a countdown to open.
Playbook updated with an Events tab explaining all event types, rules, XP values, and how Storm Chaser weather detection works.
UI changes for Run history - group. You can now clearly see how the XP is gained.
Playbook page — full game rules documentation covering XP formula, streak multipliers, levels, titles and challenge tokens. Accessible from the sidebar.
Profile page redesigned — stats and run history now in a proper two-column layout that fills the full viewport height on desktop.
Run history pagination is now dynamic — the number of cards per page adapts automatically to the available height so the list always fills the panel without overflow.

Frodo Journey updated — The Shire and MORDOR are now shown below the bar, intermediate checkpoints (Bree, Rivendell, Moria, Rauros, The Black Gate) are above at alternating heights with connecting tick lines.
Streak multiplier displayed on the profile stats page now reflects the actual value stored per run (calculated by the backend with live DB settings) instead of a hardcoded frontend approximation.
Weather data is now saved for every outdoor run — temperature, feels-like, humidity, wind speed and gusts, precipitation, snowfall, snow depth, UV index and visibility.
Data is fetched automatically after Strava sync using the run's GPS coordinates and start time via Open-Meteo — no setup required.
Historical weather is also backfilled for older runs that have location data, so your full run history gets enriched over time.
Foundation for upcoming weather-based titles and challenges — run in the rain, wind or snow and let it count.
Runs are now tagged as treadmill or outdoor — detected automatically from Strava's trainer flag.
Titles and stats that require outdoor running correctly exclude treadmill sessions — no more indoor runs inflating outdoor achievements.
Backfill applied to all existing Strava runs so historical data is accurate from day one.
Challenge system — players earn challenge tokens when leveling up and can send them to group members as 1v1 challenges.
Three challenge tiers: Minor (5–7 days), Major (7–14 days) and Legendary (14–30 days) — each with different metrics (km, runs, XP) and reward multipliers.
Winner gets an XP multiplier boost, loser takes a penalty — challenges are settled automatically when the time window closes.
Challenges page — view active challenges, pending requests, history and a W/D/L leaderboard for the group.
Active challenge widget in the top bar — shows live score and days remaining, links directly to the challenge page.
Leaderboard now shows tier flags (tokens ready to send) and a swords icon for users currently in an active challenge.
Dark mode — toggle between light and dark theme from the sidebar or top bar. RunQuest defaults to your system theme and remembers your preference.
Full design rework — consistent color hierarchy across all pages using theme-aware tokens. Outer containers use sidebar color, inner elements use background color.
Sidebar redesigned — Strava sync status with last/next sync times, mobile controls (theme toggle, notifications, profile) moved into the sidebar.
Leaderboard cards redesigned — compact layout, fixed 340px width, centered podium row, positions 4+ fill rows from center outward.
Profile page redesigned — two-column layout with run history as a right panel, avatar and position in the card header, streak stats beside the profile card.
Run history now uses pagination (7 runs per page) with previous/next navigation instead of a growing list.
Mobile optimizations — group name wraps correctly in the top bar, profile and streak cards stack vertically on small screens.
Cleaned up behind the scenes — the app now makes fewer unnecessary requests and loads data faster.
Faster startup and more stable performance during use.
Fixed a bug in the title system that could occasionally show incorrect data on page load.
Session is now automatically restored when login needs to be renewed — no more manual cookie clearing.
You stay logged in for 30 days instead of 7.
Leaderboard with XP, levels, streaks and titles.
Four competitive titles that update automatically.
Run logging — manually or via Strava.
User profiles with profile picture and personal stats.
Admin panel for user and XP management.
